title: cirsium arizonicum var. chellyense
intro: Cirsium arizonicum var. chellyense, also known as Apache thistle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing swelling and pain, and its leaves and flowers are used to treat various ailments, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Cirsium arizonicum var. chellyense is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it may also be used in topical applications, such as salves and poultices.
